# services.oink

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.oink`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.oink.apiKeyFile` | `absolute path` | Path to a file containing the API key to use when modifying DNS records. |
| `services.oink.domains` | `list of attribute set of anything` | List of attribute sets containing configuration for each domain. Each attribute set must have two attributes, one named *domain* and another named *subdomain*. The domain attribute must specify the root domain that you want to configure, and the subdomain attribute must specify its subdomain if any. If you want to configure the root domain rather than a subdomain, leave the subdomain attribute as an empty string. Additionally, you can use attributes from *services.oink.settings* to override settings per-domain. Every domain listed here *must* have API access enabled in Porkbun's control panel. |
| `services.oink.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Oink, a dynamic DNS client for Porkbun. |
| `services.oink.package` | `package` | The oink package to use. |
| `services.oink.secretApiKeyFile` | `absolute path` | Path to a file containing the secret API key to use when modifying DNS records. |
| `services.oink.settings.interval` | `integer between 60 and 172800 (both inclusive)` | Seconds to wait before sending another request. |
| `services.oink.settings.ttl` | `integer between 600 and 172800 (both inclusive)` | The TTL ("Time to Live") value to set for your DNS records. The TTL controls how long in seconds your records will be cached for. A smaller value will allow the record to update quicker. |
